{"key_event":"Civil war and militia fragmentation","reasoning":"Lebanon’s previously moderate state capacity collapsed after the Cairo Agreement, PLO autonomy, the 1975 civil-war outbreak, army splintering, and Syrian intervention, leaving only fragmented and externally constrained coercive order. Society remained highly mobilized, but its power shifted from broad civic pluralism toward armed sectarian, Palestinian, and ideological organizations, so it rose slightly as non-state capacity while becoming less accountable and more coercive.","society_change":0.3,"society_power":7.5,"state_change":-3.6,"state_power":3.0}
